title: eriogonum trichopes subsp. glandulosum
intro: Eriogonum trichopes subsp. glandulosum, also known as the sticky buckwheat or sticky eryngo, is a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrub-like perennial that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, thanks to its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in oil, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds, which can be used to soothe sore muscles, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
